signals.reasoning: BTC is liquid and orderly, but the board is still no-trade, funding is positive, and the live event veto argues against forcing fresh risk. Best treated as neutral until the shock window clears.
ETH remains slightly weaker than BTC with a mildly negative trend and no squeeze support. Under a live geopolitical veto, it still leans better as a fade than as a long.
SOL is stable but low-edge. Positive funding and a no-trade board keep it below entry threshold, especially with the event veto active.
GRASS has some upside momentum, but the signal quality is mediocre and spread is still wide. Not worth chasing through a VETO regime.
CHILLGUY is up hard, but the market is too thin and too wide, and funding is positive rather than supportive of a squeeze. Easy pass under current conditions.
GRIFFAIN has momentum, but the setup is still mixed and execution quality is mediocre. Veto conditions downgrade this to watch-only.
MORPHO has decent price strength and usable liquidity, but the board is still neutral and funding is positive. It is interesting, not actionable, while veto is on.

MON has the cleanest bearish lean among the non-major non-squeeze names: elevated positive funding, real liquidity, and a softer board profile. Not a huge edge, but still better on the short side than long.
ZEC is one of the few names with negative funding support, but the broader event veto keeps me from endorsing a fresh long. Better to wait than trade into macro noise.

TNSR still has a legitimate squeeze profile with strong momentum and negative funding, but the live geopolitical veto is exactly the kind of condition that can break continuation trades. I would not open it here.
BLUR still screens bullish mechanically, but the combination of VETO, RED macro, and wide spread makes fresh long exposure unattractive. Watchlist only until the shock regime eases.
